title: ageratina calophylla
intro: Ageratina calophylla, also known as the 'Mexican Tea' or 'False Tarragon', is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the asteraceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Ageratina calophylla include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as colds and flu. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
